articles.title: Canadian Dollar pares losses vs USD as rallying oil prices counter hawkish Fed bets
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair retreats a few pips from the Asian session high and currently trades around the 1.3860-1.3855 region, up around 0.15% for the day.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD falls as the US Dollar gains on increased risk aversion
articles.expand: GBP/USD halts its five-day winning streak, trading at a gap down around 1.3390 during the Asian hours on Monday. The risk-sensitive pair faces challenges on renewed risk aversion following the failure of the United States (US)-Iran peace talks.

articles.title: Oil prices surge after US-Iran talks collapse over the weekend
articles.expand: The negotiations between the US and Iran over the weekend failed to produce a deal. In the end, trying to come to a nuclear agreement proved to be a bridge too far. Even though it might just be a singular point, it is one massive gap that both sides are unable to compromise on. So, we're back to square one it would seem. While the two-week ceasefir...

articles.title: Gold bulls seem hesitant as failed US‑Iran talks and hawkish Fed bets support USD
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) rebounds from the $4,633-$4,632 area, or a four-day trough touched during the Asian session on Monday, and fills a major part of the weekly bearish gap amid mixed cues.

articles.title: Chart Art: NZD/USD Reversal to Gain Traction After Pullback?
articles.expand: After breaking above a long-term descending trend line, NZD/USD went into correction mode to gather more bullish energy. Can buyers still sustain the reversal?

articles.title: Crude Oil Ripped Higher Again as US-Iran Talks Collapsed & US Military Blockade Underway
articles.expand: US-Iran peace talks collapsed in Islamabad over the weekend, triggering a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and another run higher for safe-havens and crude oil.

articles.title: Oil Hits $105 but No Panic as Reality of Hormuz Blockade Falls Short of Headlines
articles.expand: Oil prices climbed back toward $105 after the latest escalation around the Strait of Hormuz, but markets have stopped well short of panic pricing. articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Middle East ceasefire hangs by a thread
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair came back to life this week, settling a handful of pips above the 1.1700 mark. Risk-on flows began late Tuesday, when United States (US) President Donald Trump withdrew his threat to destroy Iran.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Rebounds to near 1.1700 as bullish bias prevails
articles.expand: EUR/USD edges higher after opening at a gap down, trading around 1.1690 during the Asian hours on Monday. The daily chart technical analysis indicates a bullish bias, as the pair is rising within an ascending channel.

articles.title: China still has room to ease as inflation remains below target, BofA says
articles.expand: China’s inflation outlook has been nudged higher, but externally driven price pressures and weak domestic demand mean policymakers still have room to ease further. articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Strength beyond 160.00 awaited amid bullish technical setup
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair builds on gains from the past two days and opens with a bullish gap at the start of the new week, rising to the 159.85 region during the Asian session. However, intervention fears keep a lid on any further appreciation for spot prices.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Defends 200-hour EMA/38.2% Fibo. confluence below 0.7000
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair opens with a bearish gap at the start of a new week, though it lacks follow-through and recovers around 40 pips from the Asian session low levels below the 0.7000 psychological mark.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar falls further as USD haven demand counters WTI surge
articles.expand: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) is extending its pullback from two-week highs of 1.3844 against the US Dollar (USD) early Monday, with USD/CAD capitalizing on the renewed demand for the Greenback as a go-to safe-haven.

articles.title: ANZ turns hawkish on RBNZ as inflation risks build. RBNZ hike in July, September, October

articles.title: WTI jumps roughly 8% toward $100 as US blockades Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) – the US oil benchmark – has opened the week with a bullish gap, climbing roughly 8%, looking to retarget the $100 threshold.

articles.title: Lessons learned in ’70s have made the US and world economies less vulnerable to oil shocks
articles.expand: Countries have since embarked on a new course to increase their energy efficiency, reduce their dependence on Middle Eastern oil, stockpile fuel against future threats, and find and develop alternative sources of energy.

articles.title: Lessons learned in 1970s have made U.S. and global economies less vulnerable to oil shocks
articles.expand: WASHINGTON — Oil prices are once again surging in the wake of war in the Middle East, driving up the cost of gasoline, diesel and jet fuel. articles.title: NBP expands gold reserves and moves closer to the global Top 10
articles.expand: The NBP increased its gold reserves in March 2026 by about 13 tons, taking advantage of a sharp price correction. Total holdings rose to around 580 tons. Despite a lower valuation due to falling prices, the central bank continues its strategy of accumulation and is now just over 30 tons away from entering the global top 10 in gold reserves.

articles.title: USD/CAD Forecast: Bears turn cautious ahead of US-Iran talks, US CPI, Canadian jobs data
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair gains some positive traction on Friday, snapping a four-day losing streak to the 1.3800 neighborhood or over a two-week low set the previous day. Doubts over the durability of the US-Iran ceasefire support the safe-haven US Dollar (USD) and act as a tailwind for spot prices.
